# Technology & Architecture Report — myAgentic-IT-Project-team — 2026-03-07

> **Mode:** AUDIT | **Generated by:** Synthesis Agent (17) | **Cycle:** COMBO_AUDIT (TECH + UX)
> **Source phase:** Phase 2 — Architecture & Design
> **Source agents:** 05 (Software Architect), 06 (Senior Developer), 07 (DevOps Engineer), 08 (Security Architect), 09 (Data Architect), 33 (Legal Counsel)
> **Critic validation:** APPROVED (2026-03-07) — all 6 agents individually approved, 0 cross-agent contradictions

---

## 1. Summary for this team

The myAgentic-IT-Project-team webapp is a **Node.js-based internal tool** (server.js ~1,600 LOC + index.html ~2,200 LOC SPA) that manages questionnaires, decisions, and an AI-agent orchestration pipeline. The architecture is a **layerless monolith with an embedded SPA**, using zero external dependencies (only Node.js builtins: `http`, `fs`, `path`). Data is persisted as Markdown files and JSON files on the local file system, served via HTTP on localhost.

**Current state assessment:** The application is functional for single-user localhost use but carries **significant technical debt** (Tech Debt Score: 57/100 MEDIUM-HIGH, 470h remediation estimate). The audit identified **150+ findings** across architecture, code quality, DevOps, security, data, and legal dimensions. Key strengths include the zero-dependency approach (eliminates supply chain risk) and functional markdown-based persistence. Critical weaknesses include: no CI/CD pipeline (CMMI Level 0), no authentication/authorization, 47 OWASP security findings, 51 SOLID violations, file-based persistence with non-atomic writes, and no test infrastructure (0% coverage).

**Deployment context:** Currently localhost-only, which mitigates many security risks (no network exposure). However, the `QUE-DEPLOY-SCOPE` question (will this be deployed beyond localhost?) remains unanswered and is **the single most impactful variable** for the entire remediation roadmap — a "yes" would elevate all CONDITIONAL security, GDPR, and legal findings to CRITICAL.

---

## 2. Recommendations (prioritized)

| Priority | Recommendation | Source | Effort | Impact |
|----------|----------------|--------|--------|--------|
| **P0** | Answer QUE-DEPLOY-SCOPE (deployment target) — determines severity escalation for 30+ findings | Agents 05, 07, 08, 09, 33 (cross-agent consensus) | None (decision) | CRITICAL |
| **P1** | Implement content sanitization for Markdown injection prevention | Agent 08 (REC-SEC-001), Agent 09 (REC-DATA-001), confirmed by Agents 05, 06 | S (6h) | CRITICAL — 4-agent consensus on injection risk |
| **P1** | Implement atomic file writes to prevent data corruption | Agent 08 (REC-SEC-002), Agent 09 (REC-DATA-005), Agent 05 (GAP-ARCH-007) | S (2h) | HIGH — 5-agent consensus on non-atomic write risk |
| **P1** | Establish test infrastructure and baseline coverage | Agent 06 (REC-DEV-001), Agent 05 (REC-ARCH-003) | L (108h) | CRITICAL — 0% coverage blocks quality assurance |
| **P1** | Implement GitHub Actions CI pipeline | Agent 07 (REC-OPS-001) | M (16h) | HIGH — enables automated quality gates |
| **P1** | Introduce data layer abstraction (Store interface) | Agent 05 (REC-ARCH-001), Agent 06 (REC-DEV-004) | L (40h) | HIGH — architectural prerequisite for testability |
| **P1** | Add structured JSON logging | Agent 05 (REC-ARCH-006), Agent 07 (REC-OPS-002) | S (10h) | HIGH — prerequisite for observability |
| **P1** | Fix file locking race condition | Agent 06 (REC-DEV-006) | S (6h) | HIGH — data integrity risk |
| **P1** | Create and add LICENSE file | Agent 33 (REC-LEG-001) | S (1h) | HIGH — legal gap, always applicable |
| **P1** | Add secret-pattern detection on save | Agent 08 (REC-SEC-003) | S (4h) | HIGH — prevents accidental credential storage |
| **P2** | Decompose God Functions (reduce cyclomatic complexity) | Agent 06 (REC-DEV-002) | M (30h) | MEDIUM — prerequisite for maintainability |
| **P2** | Extract shared utilities (DRY elimination) | Agent 06 (REC-DEV-003) | M (22h) | MEDIUM |
| **P2** | Add in-memory cache with mtime invalidation | Agent 05 (REC-ARCH-004) | S (15h) | MEDIUM — performance improvement |
| **P2** | Implement rate limiting | Agent 08 (REC-SEC-005) | S (4h) | MEDIUM — conditional on deployment scope |
| **P2** | Add metrics endpoint and health check | Agent 07 (REC-OPS-005) | S (8h) | MEDIUM |
| **P2** | Implement multi-process file synchronization | Agent 09 (REC-DATA-003) | M (12h) | MEDIUM — conditional on multi-user scenario |
| **P2** | Establish lawful basis for processing (GDPR) | Agent 33 (REC-LEG-003) | M (16h) | HIGH — conditional on deployment + personal data |
| **P2** | Create privacy policy | Agent 33 (REC-LEG-004) | M (16h) | HIGH — conditional on deployment |
| **P2** | Add copyright notices to source files | Agent 33 (REC-LEG-002) | S (2h) | MEDIUM |
| **P2** | Implement snapshot-on-write backup strategy | Agent 07 (REC-OPS-003), Agent 05 (REC-ARCH-007) | S (10h) | MEDIUM |
| **P2** | Create security handoff context document | Agent 08 (REC-SEC-008) | S (2h) | MEDIUM |
| **P2** | Add JSDoc documentation to all public functions | Agent 06 (REC-DEV-005) | M (30h) | LOW |
| **P3** | Replace polling with Server-Sent Events | Agent 05 (REC-ARCH-005) | M (25h) | LOW — performance/UX improvement |
| **P3** | Implement data subject rights (GDPR) | Agent 33 (REC-LEG-005) | L (40h) | HIGH — conditional on deployment + personal data |
| **P3** | Replace magic numbers with named constants | Agent 06 (REC-DEV-007) | S (6h) | LOW |
| **P3** | Wrap error messages to prevent information leakage | Agent 06 (REC-DEV-008) | S (8h) | MEDIUM |

**Note:** Recommendations marked "conditional" depend on QUE-DEPLOY-SCOPE answer. If deployment remains localhost-only, these can be deprioritized substantially.

## 3. Roadmap items for this team (12 months)

| Quarter | Item | Dependent on | KPI target |
|---------|------|--------------|------------|
| **Q1** | Sprint 1: Security hardening — content sanitization, atomic writes, secret detection, security headers, LICENSE file, structured logging, file lock fix, `.gitignore`, `package.json`, CI pipeline | NONE | Markdown injection: 0 vectors; CI pipeline: active; Test setup: complete |
| **Q1** | Sprint 2: Architecture foundation — Store interface, data layer abstraction, domain model extraction, cache layer, JSON schema validation, data integrity tests | Sprint 1 complete | Store abstraction: 100% coverage; Direct `fs` calls in handlers: 0 |
| **Q2** | Sprint 3: Code quality & testing — test framework baseline (70% coverage), God function decomposition, DRY extraction, backup-on-write, integration tests, JSDoc | Sprint 2 complete (data layer prerequisite for tests) | Test coverage: ≥70% server.js; CC per function: ≤8 |
| **Q2** | Sprint 4: Observability & performance — SSE endpoint, frontend SSE integration, metrics endpoint, health check, rate limiting, CORS headers, security logging | Sprint 2 complete | API p95 latency: <50ms (cached); Data staleness: <1s (SSE) |
| **Q3** | Sprint 5: Legal & compliance — GDPR compliance package (lawful basis, privacy policy, RoPA, breach notification, DPA verification), data retention policy, PII classification | QUE-DEPLOY-SCOPE answered; legal counsel consultation | GDPR artifacts: complete (if applicable) |
| **Q3** | Sprint 6: Data governance — multi-process sync, questionnaire audit trail, data dictionary, retention enforcement | Sprint 2 (data layer) + Sprint 5 (retention policy) | Audit trail: 100% mutations logged; Data dictionary: complete |
| **Q4** | Sprint 7: Advanced security & data subject rights — DSR implementation, sector regulation mapping, pentest preparation | Sprint 5 (GDPR framework) | DSR: implemented (if applicable); Pentest: scheduled |
| **Q4** | Stabilization & quality gate — comprehensive regression testing, documentation, tech debt reduction | All prior sprints | Tech debt score: <25/100; Test coverage: ≥80% |

## 5. ⚠️ Blockers from other teams (ACTION REQUIRED)

> This team **cannot start** the items below without input or a decision from another team.

| Blocker ID | Blocking team | What is needed | Priority | Recommended deadline |
|------------|--------------|----------------|----------|---------------------|
| BLK-T-001 | **UX** | Error message framework (REC-CNT-002 from Agent 32) must be defined before Tech can implement user-facing error wrapping (REC-DEV-008, REC-SEC-001 client-side). Tech can proceed with server-side sanitization independently. | MEDIUM | Before Sprint 3 |
| BLK-T-002 | **UX** | Design token system (REC-UID-001 from Agent 12) must be established before Tech implements CSS variable changes for i18n logical properties and theme support. | MEDIUM | Before Sprint 4 |
| BLK-T-003 | **UX** | i18n string catalog structure (REC-L10N-001 from Agent 35) — Tech must agree on string externalization format before implementing the i18n lookup layer. | ADVISORY | Before Sprint 4 |

**Note:** All Tech Sprint 1–2 items have **no UX blockers** and can proceed immediately.

| Design tokens → CSS variable naming | UX (Agent 12 token system) | Shared naming convention prevents rework | LOW — align during Sprint 3 |

| GDPR technical controls → legal requirements | Legal (Agent 33) | Tech implements; Legal defines requirements (conditional on deployment scope) | HIGH if deployed; N/A if localhost |
| CI pipeline → all teams | UX, Legal | Pipeline gates apply to all code including frontend and documentation | LOW — automated once established |

## 8. Guardrails for this team

### Architecture Guardrails (Agent 05)

| ID | Rule | Violation Action |
|----|------|-----------------|
| G-ARCH-AUDIT-01 | Data Access Isolation — no direct `fs.` in handlers | Block PR; CRITICAL_FINDING |
| G-ARCH-AUDIT-02 | State Transition Centralization — all decision transitions via Decision class | Block PR; CRITICAL_FINDING |
| G-ARCH-AUDIT-03 | Test Coverage Gate — ≥80% line coverage on parsing + transitions | Block PR |
| G-ARCH-AUDIT-04 | Zero-Dependency Principle — no npm packages without ADR | Block PR; escalate |
| G-ARCH-AUDIT-05 | Structured Logging Requirement — all API requests logged JSON | Block PR; CRITICAL_FINDING |

### Code Quality Guardrails (Agent 06)

| ID | Rule | Violation Action |
|----|------|-----------------|
| G-DEV-AUDIT-01 | Max Cyclomatic Complexity — CC≤8 per function | Block PR |
| G-DEV-AUDIT-02 | Minimum Test Coverage Gate — 70% server.js, 50% frontend | Block PR |
| G-DEV-AUDIT-03 | No Direct File System in Business Logic | Block PR; escalate |
| G-DEV-AUDIT-04 | JSDoc Required for All Public Functions | Block PR |
| G-DEV-AUDIT-05 | No Duplicate Code Blocks >5 lines | Block PR |

### DevOps Guardrails (Agent 07)

| ID | Rule | Violation Action |
|----|------|-----------------|
| G-OPS-AUDIT-01 | CI Pipeline Required for All Merges | Block merge via status check |
| G-OPS-AUDIT-02 | Structured JSON Logging Format Required | Block PR |
| G-OPS-AUDIT-03 | Backup-on-Write Required for State Files | Block PR; CRITICAL_FINDING |
| G-OPS-AUDIT-04 | Version Control Governance Minimum | Escalate to admin |

### Security Guardrails (Agent 08)

| ID | Rule | Violation Action |
|----|------|-----------------|
| G-SEC-AUDIT-01 | Mandatory safePath for User-Controlled File Paths | Block PR; CRITICAL_FINDING |
| G-SEC-AUDIT-02 | Mandatory HTML Escaping for Dynamic Content | Block PR; CRITICAL_FINDING |
| G-SEC-AUDIT-03 | Content-Type Enforcement on POST Endpoints | Block PR |
| G-SEC-AUDIT-04 | No Secrets in Logs or Error Responses | Block PR |
| G-SEC-AUDIT-05 | No Network Exposure Without Authentication | Block deployment; CRITICAL_FINDING |

### Data Guardrails (Agent 09)

| ID | Rule | Violation Action |
|----|------|-----------------|
| GR-DATA-001 | Must Not Write User Text Without Markdown Escaping | Block PR; CRITICAL_FINDING |
| GR-DATA-002 | Must Validate JSON Schema on Every Read | Block PR; CRITICAL_FINDING |
| GR-DATA-003 | Must Use Atomic Write Pattern for All File Mutations | Block PR; escalate |
| GR-DATA-004 | Must Log All Data Mutations | Block PR |
| GR-DATA-005 | Must Not Store Data Beyond Retention Period | Escalate to data architect |

### Legal Guardrails (Agent 33)

| ID | Rule | Violation Action |
|----|------|-----------------|
| GR-LEG-001 | Must Not Deploy Without LICENSE File | Block deployment |
| GR-LEG-002 | Must Not Deploy to EU Without GDPR Compliance Docs | Block deployment; legal escalation |
| GR-LEG-003 | Must Not Process Personal Data Without Lawful Basis | Block; halt processing |
| GR-LEG-004 | Must Not Share Data with Processor Without DPA | Block integration |
| GR-LEG-005 | Must Not Add Dependencies Without License Review | Block PR |
